skill.name: qwen-image-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Qwen-Image-2.0 (fast), Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro (professional text rendering). Capabilities: text-to-image, multi-image editing, complex text rendering. Triggers: qwen image, qwen-image, alibaba image, dashscope image, qwen image 2, qwen image pro
skill.summary: Generate and edit images using Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models through inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis detected 53 patterns from markdown code blocks and documentation links - all are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation with bash examples for using the external inference.sh CLI tool. No security risks, malicious intent, or prompt injection attempts detected.

content.value_statement: Create professional AI-generated images and edits using Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models. Generate text-to-image art, edit with reference images, and render complex text through Claude with the inference.sh CLI.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models
Create multiple images in one request with custom resolutions
Edit images using 1-3 reference images for multi-image compositing
Render complex text in images with professional typography
Control output with negative prompts, seeds, and watermark options
Choose between fast (Qwen-Image-2) or professional (Pro) model variants
limitations: Requires separate installation of inference.sh CLI tool (infsh)
Maximum prompt length is 800 characters for main prompts
Output dimensions constrained between 512x512 and 2048x2048 pixels
External API service requires internet connectivity and account
Cannot generate images larger than 2048x2048 total pixels

use_cases.title: Marketing Visual Creation
use_cases.description: Generate product images, social media graphics, and marketing posters with text overlays using the Pro model for professional typography

use_cases.title: Concept Art and Design
use_cases.description: Create concept art, illustrations, and design variations quickly using the fast model for rapid iteration and exploration

use_cases.title: Image Editing and Compositing
use_cases.description: Edit existing images by combining elements from multiple reference images, such as changing clothing, backgrounds, or poses

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [describe subject] in [style] style
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple text-to-image generation for any subject

prompt_templates.title: Text-Heavy Poster Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a poster with title '[main title]' in [color] [size] text, subtitle '[subtitle]' in [color] below. [Background description]. Use qwen-image-2-pro model with prompt_extend: false
prompt_templates.scenario: Professional posters with precise text rendering

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Image Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit image to [desired change]. Use reference images: [Image 1 URL] shows [subject], [Image 2 URL] shows [element to apply]
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine elements from multiple images into one composition

prompt_templates.title: Controlled Generation with Negative Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate [description] with seed [number]. Negative prompt: [unwanted qualities]. Use [model] variant at [width]x[height] resolution

output_examples.input: Generate a futuristic cityscape at sunset with flying cars
output_examples.output: AI-generated image showing a sci-fi city skyline at golden hour with flying vehicles, rendered in photorealistic style

output_examples.input: Create a poster with 'Summer Sale!' in bold red text, '50% Off' in blue, beach background
output_examples.output: Vertical poster image with crisp red title text, blue subtitle text, and tropical beach scene, using Pro model for typography

output_examples.input: Make the person from image1 wear the outfit from image2
output_examples.output: Composite image combining the person from the first reference image wearing clothing from the second reference image

best_practices: Use the Pro model (qwen-image-2-pro) for text-heavy designs requiring precise typography and professional text rendering
Set prompt_extend to false when you need exact control over the generated output and don't want the model to modify your prompt
Provide specific style descriptors like 'photorealistic', 'watercolor', or 'digital art' to achieve consistent aesthetic results
anti_patterns: Don't exceed 800 characters for your main prompt or 500 characters for negative prompts - the model will truncate them
Avoid requesting output dimensions larger than 2048x2048 total pixels, as the API will reject the request
Don't use the fast model (qwen-image-2) for complex text rendering - use the Pro model instead for professional typography

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the inference.sh CLI tool. Run 'npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools' to get installation instructions. The skill itself just provides documentation for using this external tool.

faq.question: What is the difference between qwen-image-2 and qwen-image-2-pro?
faq.answer: The standard qwen-image-2 model is faster and good for general image generation. The Pro model offers professional text rendering, fine-grained realism, and enhanced semantic adherence, making it better for posters and text-heavy designs.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images with this tool?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide 1-3 reference images to guide the editing process. Use the reference_images parameter in your input to specify which elements from each image should be combined or modified.

faq.question: What image sizes can I generate?
faq.answer: You can generate images between 512x512 and 2048x2048 total pixels. Common sizes include 1024x1024 for square images, 1536x1024 for landscape, or 1024x1536 for portrait orientation.

faq.question: How do I get consistent results across multiple generations?
faq.answer: Use the seed parameter with a specific integer value (0-2147483647). Running the same prompt with the same seed will produce identical output, allowing for reproducible results.

faq.question: What is the negative prompt used for?
faq.answer: The negative prompt specifies content you want to avoid in the generated image, such as 'low resolution, blurry, deformed, oversaturated'. This helps improve output quality by explicitly excluding unwanted characteristics.
